gaps.snippet: use std :: fmt ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A byte offset into the input: the **locus** of a serialization failure (C1 /"] # [doc = " RFC-0013 I1). Named `none` (0) when the locus cannot be determined (e.g. the"] # [doc = " first byte of a completely empty input)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct ByteOffset (pub u64) ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The explicit error set for (de)serialization failures (C1/RFC-0013; spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every variant carries a locus (byte offset or field path) so the caller can"] # [doc = " surface *where* the decode failed — never a locationless \"parse error\" (G2/C3)."] # [doc = " An overrun yields `BudgetExceeded`, never an OOM or a hang (C6/ADR-015)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 compliance"] # [doc = " No variant of `SerError` is a sentinel, a clamp, or a partial-result indicator."] # [doc = " The presence of a `SerError` means the entire decode is rejected; the caller"] # [doc = " never receives a partially-filled `Value`."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum SerError { # [doc = " The input ended before a complete value was decoded."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `at` is the byte offset where the input terminated (the last byte"] # [doc = " successfully consumed before the truncation was detected)."] Truncated { # [doc = " Byte offset of the truncation."] at : ByteOffset , } , # [doc = " The bytes at `at` do not conform to the wire/JSON grammar."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `why` is a human-readable explanation of the grammar rule that was"] # [doc = " violated (G11 dual projection — machine-parseable in the tag, human-"] # [doc = " legible in the description)."] Malformed { # [doc = " Byte offset of the malformed datum."] at : ByteOffset , # [doc = " Why the datum was rejected."] why : String , } , # [doc = " An unrecognized `Repr`, constructor, or `Meta` tag was encountered at `path`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carrying the rejected `tag` name enables the caller to log it and report"] # [doc = " it exactly (G11); an unknown tag is never silently skipped (C1/G2)."] UnknownTag { # [doc = " Structural path to the field that held the unknown tag."] path : FieldPath , # [doc = " The unrecognized tag string."] tag : String , } , # [doc = " A field decoded successfully but violates a value-model invariant (RFC-0001"] # [doc = " §4.3 — e.g. payload length ≠ repr width, bound delta ∉ \\[0,1\\])."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The invariant name is carried in `why` so the caller can surface it."] OutOfDomain { # [doc = " Structural path to the violating field."] path : FieldPath , # [doc = " The violated invariant."] why : String , } , # [doc = " A declared decode budget (depth limit, enumeration ceiling — ADR-015) was"] # [doc = " exceeded. `kind` names which budget: `\"depth\"`, `\"enum\"`, `\"alloc\"`, etc."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " An overrun is an explicit error, never a hang or an OOM (C6)."] BudgetExceeded { # [doc = " The budget kind that was exceeded (e.g. `\"depth\"`, `\"enum\"`)."] kind : String , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `SerError` variant `Truncated` named field(s) `at` emitted positionally as `Truncated(ByteOffset)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: impl fmt :: Display for SerError { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { SerError :: Truncated { at } => write ! (f , "truncated input at {at}") , SerError :: Malformed { at , why } => write ! (f , "malformed input at {at}: {why}") , SerError :: UnknownTag { path , tag } => { write ! (f , "unknown tag {tag:?} at {path}") } SerError :: OutOfDomain { path , why } => { write ! (f , "value out of domain at {path}: {why}") } SerError :: BudgetExceeded { kind } => { write ! (f , "decode budget exceeded ({kind})") } } } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The explicit error set for byte-movement failures (C1/RFC-0013; spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every variant is a declared, inspectable failure mode — never a silent partial"] # [doc = " read (C1) and never a hang or OOM (C6)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum IoError { # [doc = " The source/sink closed before the requested number of bytes were moved."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `read` is the count of bytes that *were* moved before the EOF — enabling"] # [doc = " the caller to report the shortfall precisely (RFC-0013 I1)."] UnexpectedEof { # [doc = " Bytes successfully read before the EOF."] read : ByteCount , } , # [doc = " The underlying substrate refused the operation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `why` carries the refusal reason (G11 dual projection); for the in-memory"] # [doc = " substrate this is always the explicit reason the substrate declined."] Refused { # [doc = " Why the operation was refused."] why : String , } , # [doc = " A bounded io/alloc effect budget was exceeded (RFC-0014 §4.5 / C6)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `kind` names which budget: `\"io\"`, `\"alloc\"`, etc."] EffectBudget { # [doc = " The budget kind that was exceeded."] kind : String , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " `SerError::Truncated` Display includes the byte offset (RFC-0013 I1 / G11)."] # [doc = " Guard: a display that drops the offset makes this fail."] # [test] fn ser_error_truncated_display_includes_offset () { let e = SerError :: Truncated { at : ByteOffset (42) } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("42") , "display must include the byte offset") ; } # [doc = " `SerError::Malformed` Display includes both the offset and the reason."] # [doc = " Guard: dropping either field makes this fail."] # [test] fn ser_error_malformed_display_includes_offset_and_why () { let e = SerError :: Malformed { at : ByteOffset (7) , why : "unexpected 0xFF in bit string" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("7") , "display must include the offset") ; assert ! (s . contains ("0xFF") , "display must include the rejection reason") ; } # [doc = " `SerError::UnknownTag` Display includes both the path and the tag."] # [doc = " Guard: dropping either field makes this fail."] # [test] fn ser_error_unknown_tag_display_includes_path_and_tag () { let e = SerError :: UnknownTag { path : FieldPath :: from_static ("repr/kind") , tag : "Quantum" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("repr/kind") , "must include the field path") ; assert ! (s . contains ("Quantum") , "must include the rejected tag") ; } # [doc = " `SerError::OutOfDomain` Display includes both the path and the reason."] # [test] fn ser_error_out_of_domain_display_includes_path_and_why () { let e = SerError :: OutOfDomain { path : FieldPath :: from_static ("meta/bound/delta") , why : "delta 1.5 ∉ [0,1]" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("meta/bound/delta") , "must include the path") ; assert ! (s . contains ("1.5") , "must include the why") ; } # [doc = " `SerError::BudgetExceeded` Display includes the budget kind."] # [test] fn ser_error_budget_exceeded_display_includes_kind () { let e = SerError :: BudgetExceeded { kind : "enum" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("enum") , "must include the budget kind") ; } # [doc = " `SerError` implements `std::error::Error` (compile-time check)."] # [test] fn ser_error_is_std_error () { let e = SerError :: Truncated { at : ByteOffset (0) } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " `IoError::UnexpectedEof` Display includes the byte count (RFC-0013 I1)."] # [test] fn io_error_unexpected_eof_display_includes_count () { let e = IoError :: UnexpectedEof { read : ByteCount (15) , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("15") , "must include the byte count") ; } # [doc = " `IoError::Refused` Display includes the reason."] # [test] fn io_error_refused_display_includes_why () { let e = IoError :: Refused { why : "substrate is consumed" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("consumed") , "must include the refusal reason") ; } # [doc = " `IoError::EffectBudget` Display includes the budget kind."] # [test] fn io_error_effect_budget_display_includes_kind () { let e = IoError :: EffectBudget { kind : "alloc" . to_owned () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("alloc") , "must include the budget kind") ; } # [doc = " `IoError` implements `std::error::Error`."] # [test] fn io_error_is_std_error () { let e = IoError :: Refused { why : "test" . to_owned () , } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " `ReadValueError` distinguishes the two error classes (C3)."] # [doc = " Guard: making From<IoError> and From<SerError> both map to the same variant"] # [doc = " would collapse the discrimination — this test catches that."] # [test] fn read_value_error_distinguishes_io_and_ser () { let io_err : ReadValueError = IoError :: Refused { why : "test" . to_owned () , } . into () ; let ser_err : ReadValueError = SerError :: Truncated { at : ByteOffset (0) } . into () ; assert ! (matches ! (io_err , ReadValueError :: Io (_))) ; assert ! (matches ! (ser_err , ReadValueError :: Ser (_))) ; assert_ne ! (io_err , ser_err) ; } # [doc = " `ReadValueError` implements `std::error::Error` and exposes `source()`."] # [test] fn read_value_error_has_source () { let e : ReadValueError = IoError :: Refused { why : "x" . to_owned () , } . into () ; assert ! (std :: error :: Error :: source (& e) . is_some () , "source() must chain to the underlying IoError") ; } }
